summary: Phase 3 testing: 17/23 backend tests passed; one critical backend root-cause bug blocks all new registrations after the first one. Frontend UI elements (login/register/forgot dialogs, data-testids, toasts) all render correctly.

critical.issue: DuplicateKeyError on telegram_id:null prevents ANY new user from registering after a single existing user has telegram_id=null. The `users.telegram_id` index is `unique:true, sparse:true`, BUT MongoDB's sparse only skips documents where the field is missing — it does NOT skip explicit null values. The User pydantic model emits `telegram_id: null` via model_dump(), so every register insert collides with the seed user `testuser_phase3` (telegram_id:null). The endpoint catches DuplicateKeyError and mis-reports it as 'Bu kullanıcı adı zaten alınmış' (409) — misleading because the username was actually unique.

DuplicateKeyError catch (line 684) returns generic 'username taken' message regardless of which unique field actually clashed (username, telegram_id, etc). Should inspect the error key or differentiate.

integration_issues.issue: Backend returns 409 'Bu kullanıcı adı zaten alınmış' even for fresh unique usernames — root cause is the telegram_id sparse-null bug above. Toast displays correctly.

action_items: FIX (critical): Replace the `telegram_id` sparse index with a partial filter index. Drop existing index and recreate: `db.users.create_index('telegram_id', unique=True, partialFilterExpression={'telegram_id': {'$exists': True, '$type': 'string'}})`. Also drop the existing index manually (mongosh: `db.users.dropIndex('telegram_id_1')`) on startup or via migration, otherwise create_index is a no-op.

FIX (alt): When inserting new users without telegram_id, exclude the field entirely (e.g. `doc = user_obj.model_dump(exclude_none=True)` or pop `telegram_id` if None) so the sparse index actually skips it.
FIX (high): Differentiate DuplicateKeyError causes — currently any duplicate (username, telegram_id, email) returns 'kullanıcı adı zaten alınmış'.
Same null-collision risk exists for `email` (sparse index) if a future feature inserts users without email — pre-empt with partialFilterExpression there too.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py L952: `telegram_id` index uses sparse, but sparse doesn't skip explicit null. Use partialFilterExpression.
server.py L683-685: catch DuplicateKeyError → always 'kullanıcı adı zaten alınmış' is misleading. Inspect e.details.keyPattern.
server.py L26: `os.environ['MONGO_URL']` will crash startup if missing — acceptable fail-fast, OK.
server.py L660 (register): `validate_phone` runs after email Pydantic validation but before username uniqueness check — order is fine but combine validators into one helper to DRY.
server.py file is 976 lines; recommend splitting auth, telegram, clips, leaderboard into separate routers.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on rendered UI elements (registration blocked by backend bug)

test_credentials: Pre-seeded: testuser_phase3 / sifre123. Note: this user has telegram_id:null which is the exact cause of the critical bug.
seed_data_creation: Tests insert TEST_/pyt_ prefixed users + reset codes; cleanup fixture removes them on teardown. Some inserts succeeded (those done via direct mongosh insert bypass the API), others failed via API due to the null-collision bug.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Once main agent applies the partialFilterExpression fix AND drops the existing telegram_id_1 index in DB (create_index is a no-op if the index exists with different options), re-run /app/backend/tests/test_phase3_auth.py. Expect all 23 tests to pass. UI register flow already wired; toast displays backend errors verbatim.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE: MongoDB `sparse: true` skips documents where the indexed field is missing, but does NOT skip documents where the field exists with value null. The User model emits telegram_id explicitly as null. The seed user testuser_phase3 already occupies telegram_id:null in the unique index. Every subsequent register insert collides → DuplicateKeyError → endpoint returns generic 409 'username taken'. REPRODUCTION: `curl -X POST $API/api/auth/register -d '{"username":"<any new>","password":"sifre123","email":"x@y.com","phone":"+905551112233"}'` returns 409. Verified backend log shows `E11000 dup key error ... index: telegram_id_1 dup key: { telegram_id: null }`. MITIGATION: (a) Recreate the index with `partialFilterExpression={'telegram_id': {'$type':'string'}}` AND drop the legacy unique index first, OR (b) `model_dump(exclude_none=True)` so the field is omitted when null. Option (a) is more robust because future users with email=null would hit the same trap with the email sparse index.
